sprint: Magic Card Home Scroll Fix + My Projects Delete + Companion Load Retry
task: Three real, user-reported bugs from a fresh screenshot pair plus one blunt follow-up line: 'screen 1: scroll, screen 2: self projects no delete option. overall on studio screen sometimes the companion does not load.' (1) Magic Card Home ('View My Card,' reached via the header badge) showed a genuine scrollbar -- root cause: the previous sprint's _fitAwakenStageToAvailableSpace() fit-and-shrink function was only ever wired into the Awakening ceremony's five screens, never into Magic Card Home's own separate .magic-card-home-panel, even though that panel already had its own real max-height/overflow-y bound with nothing measuring content against it. Fixed by calling the same fit function from openHome() (panel and stage both the home panel itself, since IT -- not the outer .magic-card-panel -- is the genuinely bounded/scrolling container in this mode) plus a matching ResizeObserver, mirroring the Awakening screens' own wiring exactly. Extending real coverage to Home's own content required one new escalation step (the function's own doc comment updated from 'four escalating steps' to five): the stories thumbnail grid, a real block of content none of the Awakening screens have, is shrunk by reducing the GRID's own overall width (never cropped/clipped) -- since every thumbnail keeps aspect-ratio:4/5, a narrower grid produces proportionally shorter thumbnails automatically, the identical 'shrink width, let aspect-ratio do the rest' technique the pre-existing art-canvases step already used. .magic-card-home-name/-since/-stories-label/-stories-grid's own fixed margins/gaps were also converted to the shared calc(var(--awaken-density,1) * Npx) pattern so the function's last-resort density fallback can compress them too. Verified via a fresh Playwright sweep across 5 realistic viewport heights (560-900px): zero overflow (scrollHeight===clientHeight) at every one after the fix (560px alone still had a real 28px residual before the stories-grid escalation step was added, closed by adding it); confirmed at realistic phone width (380px) too; a genuinely extreme, smaller-than-any-real-device 320x420 viewport confirmed the escalation correctly engages (density hits its 0.3 floor, grid width genuinely shrinks) and gracefully falls back to the panel's own pre-existing overflow-y:auto safety net rather than breaking, matching the same disclosed precedent already established for the Awakening screens' own fit function. (2) The 'Continue a Project' (My Projects) screen's cards had no delete option at all -- js/creationFlow.js's _projectCard() was a bare <button> with a single 'open this project' click handler and nothing else. Following the exact role='button' <div> + Enter/Space keydown handler + stopPropagation() pattern this codebase already established once for World Builder's own project cards (a native <button> can't legally contain a nested clickable action), the card is now a div with a real Delete button inside it -- a window.confirm() gate, then CreatorProjectStore.remove(record.id), then either re-rendering My Projects (more remain) or returning to Screen 1 (none do, avoiding a dead-end empty grid). The new inner button needed the same !important width/height/margin overrides this codebase has fixed repeatedly for a nested button caught by the global button:not(...){width:100%} cascade rule (Object Strip cards, the Publish Studio close button, Doodle's pen swatches, and now this). Verified via Playwright: deleting one of three cards removes exactly that one from both the DOM and the real vihustudio-projects storage record while staying on My Projects (confirming stopPropagation works -- a bubbled click would have also fired the card's own 'open project' handler and navigated away); deleting the last remaining project correctly returns to Screen 1; declining the confirm() dialog leaves the project completely untouched. (3) 'Sometimes the companion does not load' -- traced, not guessed: js/companionDirector.js's _mountEntity() called engine.load(id) with zero retry, and CompanionEngine.load()'s own fetch(companion.json) has no retry of its own either; since CompanionDirector.init() is called exactly once per boot (js/app.js's _beginBoot()) with nothing anywhere re-triggering it, a single transient fetch hiccup (a cold static-host request, a brief connectivity blip -- exactly the class of failure real users on real networks hit) permanently left the companion absent for that entire session, with zero visible error. Fixed with one small, bounded retry-with-backoff (_loadWithRetry, 2 total attempts, 700ms delay) wrapped around the existing engine.load(id) call inside _mountEntity -- js/companionEngine.js itself is untouched (confirmed via git diff, matching every prior Companion sprint's own verification discipline). Verified via Playwright route interception: a simulated first-attempt failure (a real aborted fetch, not a mock) on companion.json is confirmed recovered by the retry -- the companion widget still mounts and is visible, reaching the real Workspace successfully; a separate, persistent-failure simulation (every attempt fails) confirms the retry stays correctly bounded at exactly 2 attempts rather than looping forever, with the companion correctly staying absent and zero page errors, Studio remaining fully functional either way. Full regression across the entire existing seven-item-batch verification suite, the sky-recognition-challenge/voice suite, and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this batch touches zero Builder files) passes unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/magicCardUI.js, js/companionDirector.js, js/creationFlow.js, css/style.css, and index.html.
